questions.question: Which artist is widely known as the 'King of Pop'?
Which British musicianfamously wrote the lyric, 'Life is what happens to you while you're busy making other plans'?
Which iconic American country music singer-songwriter famously observed, 'If you want the rainbow, you gotta put up with the rain'?
In which year did the original Woodstock festival take place?
Who was the original drummer for The Beatles before Ringo Starr?
Who was the lead singer of the rock band Queen?
Who produced Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' album?
Who was the lead singer and guitarist of Nirvana?

Who designed the iconic 'Tongue and Lips' logo for the Rolling Stones?
Which album holds the record for the highest worldwide sales of all time?
Which artist created the alter ego Ziggy Stardust?
In which year was Metallica's self-titled 'Black Album' released?
What was the name of Bob Marley's backing band?
Which Pink Floyd album features a prism on the cover artwork?
questions.answer: The Dark Side of the Moon

Hailing from Liverpool, England, this legendary 1960s rock band consisting of John, Paul, George, and Ringo became the best-selling musical act in history.
Known worldwide as the 'King of Pop', this iconic American singer and dancer released 'Thriller', the best-selling album of all time.
Fronted by the legendary Freddie Mercury, this British rock band is world-famous for massive stadium anthems like 'Bohemian Rhapsody' and 'We Will Rock You'.
This global pop superstar shattered industry records with her massive career-spanning 'Eras Tour' and hit albums like '1989' and 'Midnights'.
Universally referred to as the 'King of Rock and Roll', this hip-swiveling 1950s icon culturalized music with hits like 'Hound Dog' and 'Jailhouse Rock'.
This legendary 18th-century Austrian child prodigy and classical composer wrote timeless masterpieces like 'The Marriage of Figaro' and 'Twinkle, Twinkle, Little Star'.
A standard modern acoustic piano features a total layout of how many black and white keys?
Originally rising to fame as the frontwoman of Destiny's Child, this cultural icon became a solo powerhouse known to fans worldwide as 'Queen Bey'.
This legendary Jamaican singer-songwriter popularized reggae music globally with his band The Wailers and iconic songs like 'Three Little Birds'.
How many strings are found on a standard, traditional acoustic or electric guitar?
This legendary British piano-playing singer-songwriter is famous for hit songs like 'Rocket Man' and his touching tribute 'Candle in the Wind'.
Despite tragically losing his hearing later in life, this iconic German composer created masterpieces like his famous 'Fifth Symphony'.
Also known by his alter ego Slim Shady, this Detroit rapper dominated the 2000s with hits like 'Lose Yourself' and 'Without Me'.
Fronted by the energetic Mick Jagger, this legendary British rock band has been performing together for over 60 years and is famous for the hit '(I Can't Get No) Satisfaction'.
An absolute icon of visual reinvention and pop culture, this singer is widely recognized as the 'Queen of Pop' with hits like 'Like a Virgin'.
This Canadian rapper and singer, often called 'Champagne Papi', has dominated the modern streaming era with hits like 'God's Plan' and 'Hotline Bling'.
Fronted by the late Kurt Cobain, this 1990s Seattle band brought grunge music into the mainstream with their anthem 'Smells Like Teen Spirit'.
This British powerhouse singer named her record-breaking studio albums '19', '21', '25', and '30' after the ages she was when she wrote them.
In August 1969, nearly half a million people gathered on a dairy farm in upstate New York for this legendary, defining rock festival of the counterculture era.
This Swedish pop group swept the world in the 1970s with infectious hits like 'Dancing Queen' and 'Mamma Mia'.
This young alternative-pop sensation swept all four major categories at the 2020 Grammys with her debut album made alongside her brother, Finneas.
Known affectionately as 'The Man in Black', this deep-voiced country music icon famously performed live concerts for inmates at Folsom Prison.
This Barbadian singer and business mogul dominated the global charts with massive pop and R&B hits like 'Umbrella', 'Diamonds', and 'Work'.
Featuring the iconic guitar work of Jimmy Page and vocals of Robert Plant, this legendary hard rock band recorded the heavy masterpiece 'Stairway to Heaven'.
